real_meaning: Contains artificial sweeteners or hidden Maltodextrin.
biological_impact: Maltodextrin has a higher Glycemic Index (GI: 110) than pure table sugar (GI: 65), causing massive insulin spikes. Artificial sweeteners like Aspartame can disrupt the gut microbiome and potentially increase long-term cravings.
how_to_spot: Look for Maltodextrin, Sucralose, Aspartame, or INS 955 / INS 951 in the ingredient list.

real_meaning: Usually 90% refined flour (Maida) with a tiny sprinkling of cheap seeds for visual effect.
biological_impact: Refined flour strips away fiber and nutrients, acting exactly like sugar in the bloodstream. It causes rapid fat storage and metabolic syndrome.
how_to_spot: Check the first ingredient on the back. If it says 'Refined Wheat Flour' or 'Maida', it is junk food. A true whole grain product will list '100% Whole Wheat' as the ONLY flour.

real_meaning: A sugar-filled product with a microscopic spray of synthetic Vitamin C.
biological_impact: The massive sugar content actually SUPPRESSES your immune system by reducing the phagocytic index of white blood cells for hours after consumption, completely overriding any benefit of the added vitamins.
how_to_spot: Check the 'Total Sugars' per 100g. If it is over 10g, the product will harm your immunity, regardless of the claims on the front box.

real_meaning: Contains less than 2% fruit puree or concentrate, functioning mainly as a flavor vehicle.
biological_impact: Fruit concentrate is essentially just fructose syrup stripped of fiber. It hits the liver directly, promoting non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) and visceral fat.
how_to_spot: Read the ingredient percentages. If 'Fruit Pulp/Concentrate' is listed at 2% or 5%, you are paying for flavored sugar water.

real_meaning: Still highly processed and often sprayed with low-quality inflammatory seed oils.
biological_impact: Extruded 'baked' snacks break down instantly into glucose in the mouth. Furthermore, they are often coated in Palmolein or Refined Sunflower oil, leading to high Omega-6 ratios which trigger systemic inflammation.
how_to_spot: Look for 'Edible Vegetable Oil (Palmolein)' or 'Refined Oil' in the ingredients. Check the calorie count; it's often identical to fried chips.

real_meaning: Legally allowed to contain up to 0.5g of trans fat per serving, and uses ridiculously tiny serving sizes to cheat the math.
biological_impact: Trans fats are artificial fats that your body literally cannot process. They permanently lodge in arterial walls, driving atherosclerosis and coronary artery disease more aggressively than any other lipid.
how_to_spot: If the ingredients list 'Partially Hydrogenated Vegetable Oil', 'Hydrogenated Fat', or 'Vanaspati', it contains dangerous trans fats, regardless of the 'Zero' claim on the front.

real_meaning: Uses the cheapest, least bioavailable forms of synthetic vitamins that your body cannot absorb.
biological_impact: For example, using Calcium Carbonate (poor absorption, risk of kidney stones) instead of Calcium Citrate, or synthetic Folic Acid instead of natural Folate. It provides a false sense of security while delivering zero real nutrition.
how_to_spot: Look at the specific chemical names in the bracket next to the vitamins. If it says 'Ferrous Fumarate' or 'Calcium Carbonate', it's low quality.

real_meaning: Derived from a 'natural' source originally, but heavily processed in a lab using chemical solvents.
biological_impact: While legally 'natural', these extracts often contain hidden chemical carriers or preservatives. Some 'natural' colors like Annatto (INS 160b) or Carmine (INS 120 - crushed bugs) can still trigger allergic reactions or IBS in sensitive individuals.
how_to_spot: Look for generic 'Added Flavors (Natural & Nature Identical)' or INS codes. 'Nature Identical' means chemically synthesized to match nature.

real_meaning: Contains mostly liquid glucose, invert sugar, or malt extract, with a tiny drop of honey for marketing.
biological_impact: Even if it is 100% real jaggery, biologically, your liver cannot tell the difference between jaggery and refined sugar. It all breaks down into glucose and fructose, causing the exact same insulin spike.
how_to_spot: Check the ingredient list order. If 'Invert Sugar', 'Liquid Glucose', or 'Sugar' comes before 'Honey/Jaggery', you're being scammed. Also check 'Total Sugars'.

real_meaning: Uses low-quality protein sources like soy isolate or wheat gluten, or requires you to drink 2 glasses of milk to actually get the protein.
biological_impact: Low-quality plant isolates often lack a complete amino acid profile, meaning poor muscle protein synthesis. Additionally, the high sugar content completely offsets the health benefits of the protein.
how_to_spot: Look at 'Protein per 100g'. Anything less than 15g-20g per 100g is NOT high protein. Look for 'Whey Protein Concentrate/Isolate' or 'Pea Protein' over 'Soy Protein'.
